Patch Set 4:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/29470/

Patch Set 4:

It would be good to have patches that "just do one thing". For example that "one thing" could be "unifdefing __KERNEL__ in lnet/".  Doing it this way it's much easier to get right and much easier to review. When I push another version of this patch it will be just the unifdefing. Then you, Amir, or I can push these tiny cleanups.

Patch Set 4: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/29470/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 4:

Onyx Autotest started optional review-zfs session.

Patch Set 4:

Onyx Autotest started enforced review-ldiskfs session.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Agree, a patch should do just one thing. But whatever the one thing a patch does, the resulting code should be clean. And in this case, I don't think totally unnecessary goto's is clean.

That's just my opinion. I'm sure everyone differs. I don't care how it's done. I just want to make sure it IS done. In this case I tend to believe it'd be way easier to clean up such places while the ifdef's are being removed, rather than to (remember to) come back later and examine the code to do such cleanups.

parents.subject: LU-5957 mdt: Update MDT flags after layout swap

commit.subject: LU-2675 lnet: assume a kernel build
commit.message:
LU-2675 lnet: assume a kernel build

In lnet/lnet/ and lnet/selftest/ assume a kernel build (assume that
__KERNEL__ is defined). Remove some common code only needed for user
space LNet.

Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Change-Id: I79d6f50bac895116628c93c35e23f64dd102780f
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13121
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Amir Shehata <amir.shehata@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
